stories.headline: Canada’s Air Defense Revival: Lessons from Ukraine & Middle East Wars Drive Interest in IRIS-T, NASAMS & CAMM Systems
stories.summary: Canada is reviving its air defense capabilities, driven by lessons from the Ukraine and Middle East conflicts. The Canadian Armed Forces is exploring new air defense systems, including IRIS-T, NASAMS, and CAMM. Recently, the Canadian government donated a National Advanced Surface-to-Air Missile System (NASAMS) to Ukraine to strengthen its air defense against Russian attacks. The Canadian military is considering medium-range air defense systems, such as NASAMS and IRIS-T, to protect large regions and troop groupings from cruise missiles and aircraft. A bid for the Bodluv MR Programme is set to launch in April 2024, with Kongsberg/Raytheon and MBDA competing for the contract. The Canadian government plans to spend billions of dollars on a new air defense system, aiming to enhance its defensive capabilities against emerging threats.

stories.headline: From oil shock to nuclear surge in Asia
stories.summary: Oil prices surged after US-Iran talks failed, with US President Donald Trump announcing a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. This move is expected to accelerate a shift towards cleaner technologies in Asia and Europe. The region is already investing in alternatives such as solar panels, wind turbines, and electric vehicles. Meanwhile, China is expanding its nuclear force and increasing military pressure against Taiwan, according to a Pentagon report. China has also strengthened its ties with Russia over the past year. Additionally, a Chinese researcher's death after "hostile questioning" by US law enforcement has prompted anger in Beijing. The death has sparked tensions between the US and China, with Beijing demanding answers about the circumstances surrounding the researcher's death. Asia is expected to play a significant role in the global transition to cleaner energy.

stories.headline: Pope Leo issues warning on democracy after Trump criticism
stories.summary: Pope Leo XIV warned of the risk of democracies sliding into "majoritarian tyranny" in a letter issued by the Vatican. The warning came two days after US President Donald Trump criticized the pontiff on social media, calling him "weak" due to his opposition to the US-Israeli war in Iran and critiques of militarism. In the letter addressed to the Pontifical Academy of Social Sciences, Pope Leo stated that democracy risks becoming a "majoritarian tyranny" if not rooted in moral law. The pope's comments highlight the tension between the Vatican and the US administration, particularly regarding military actions and social issues. The Vatican's letter serves as a response to the criticism, emphasizing the importance of moral foundations in democratic systems to prevent the erosion of democratic values.

stories.headline: US to allow waiver on Iran oil to expire, administration sources say
stories.summary: The Trump administration will allow a 30-day waiver on Iranian oil sanctions to expire later this week. The waiver, which temporarily allowed the purchase of certain Iranian crude oil, will not be renewed. This move is part of the US effort to tighten its blockade on Iranian exports. The expiration of the waiver will end a temporary measure that had eased global supply pressures by allowing significant volumes of oil to reach markets. The US has a range of penalties it can apply to institutions involved in illicit activities with Iran, including secondary sanctions. The waiver on Russian oil at sea, which was similar, expired on Saturday and was not renewed. The US Treasury Department confirmed that it does not plan to renew the temporary easing of sanctions on Iranian oil.

stories.headline: Apple Launches New All-in-One Apple Business Platform for Device Management, Email, and Customer Engagement
stories.summary: Apple has launched its new all-in-one Apple Business platform, which unifies device management, productivity tools, and customer outreach features. The platform includes built-in mobile device management, allowing businesses to configure employee groups, device settings, security, and apps using Blueprints. Apple Business also features business email, calendar, and customer engagement tools. The service is designed to replace several of Apple's existing business-focused products, including Apple Business Essentials. The launch includes a refreshed Apple Business web portal and accompanying app, providing a consolidated solution for businesses to manage their devices and operations. The platform aims to simplify device management and enhance productivity for businesses, with easy setup and configuration options. Apple Business is now available, offering a streamlined approach to managing Apple devices and services in a business setting.

stories.headline: Microsoft announces huge big increases for Surface laptops
stories.summary: Microsoft has announced significant price increases for its Surface laptops. The 13.8-inch flagship Surface Laptop, initially priced at $999, now costs $1,499. The 15-inch model's starting price has increased to $1,599. These hikes represent increases of up to $500 for flagship models, with laptops now starting at over $1,000. The price increases come at a time when the cost of PC parts is expected to decrease due to easing pressure from the AI bubble. The Surface price hikes are part of a broader trend, with Microsoft implementing increases across its Surface device range. The new prices are effective, making deals on existing models more valuable, particularly for those looking to purchase a Surface Laptop with advanced features like the Snapdragon X Elite.

stories.headline: Fedora Linux 44 Update Gets Delayed to April 21
stories.summary: Fedora Linux 44's release has been delayed from its initial target date of April 14 to at least April 21 due to final blocker bugs. The Fedora Project maintains a six-month release schedule, with new versions typically released in spring and fall. Fedora 44 was planned for release between April 14-21, bringing significant updates including the latest GNOME 50 desktop components, a unified KDE out-of-the-box experience with Plasma 6.6, and Linux kernel 6.19. The Fedora 44 Beta release was made available on schedule on March 10, working towards the official release. The delay is minor, and the new expected launch date is April 21, with the release aiming to deliver the latest and greatest updates across desktops and tools. The Fedora Project is working to resolve the blocker bugs to meet the new release target.

stories.headline: Apple Releases Second iOS 26.5, iPadOS 26.5, tvOS 26.5 and watchOS 26.5 Public Betas
stories.summary: Apple has released the second public betas of iOS 26.5, iPadOS 26.5, tvOS 26.5, and watchOS 26.5 for testing purposes. The public betas, which come a day after the developer betas, are available through the Apple Beta Software Program. The builds for the public betas are the same as those released to developers, with iOS 26.5 beta 2 identified as 23F5054d (IPSW) and 23F5054h (OTA). Public beta testers can expect to receive the updates in the coming days. The new releases appear to have minimal changes compared to the previous beta. The public beta 2 releases follow the initial developer beta releases, which were made available a day earlier. macOS Tahoe 26.5 Public Beta 2 is also expected to arrive in the coming days.

stories.headline: Roy Keane judges Man United’s European chances after Leeds loss
stories.summary: Manchester United's Premier League top-five hopes are in doubt after a 2-1 defeat to Leeds United. Roy Keane criticized the team's performance, stating they "started slowly" and only improved after going down to 10 men following a red card to Lisandro Martinez. Keane specifically targeted midfielders Casemiro and Manuel Ugarte, saying they "weren't sprinting" and their effort was "too little, too late." The loss was Manchester United interim manager Michael Carrick's first defeat at Old Trafford. The team is now focusing on securing a Champions League spot, with Bruno Fernandes emphasizing the importance of a strong finish. The defeat has raised concerns about the team's European chances, with Keane urging a strong finish to the season.

stories.headline: Javokhir Sindarov wins Candidates 2026, sets up World Chess Championship clash
stories.summary: Javokhir Sindarov has won the FIDE Candidates Tournament 2026, securing the title with a round to spare. The 20-year-old Uzbek grandmaster delivered a flawless campaign, earning the right to challenge reigning world champion D Gukesh for the crown later this year. Sindarov's victory sets up a historic under-21 world title match, marking a significant milestone in his career. He finished the tournament with an impressive performance, extending his lead with a series of wins, including a sixth victory that took his overall total to an unbeaten eight points out of 10. Sindarov's win guarantees a World Chess Championship clash with Gukesh, scheduled to take place later this year. The matchup is highly anticipated, pitting two young and talented players against each other for the top title in chess.

stories.headline: Javier Mascherano out as Inter Miami manager in stunning twist
stories.summary: Javier Mascherano has stepped down as Inter Miami's manager, the club announced on Tuesday. The decision comes as a surprise, just four months after leading the team to their first MLS Cup title. Inter Miami is currently off to a 3-1-3 start and sitting in third place in the Eastern Conference, although they are winless in their first two home matches. Mascherano's departure is a stunning twist for the reigning champions, who had achieved success under his leadership. The club announced that Mascherano has decided to bring his tenure as Head Coach to an end, effective immediately. The move comes after a successful stint with the team, which included winning the MLS Cup title with high-profile players like Lionel Messi.

stories.headline: Etzebeth among group of Sharks out for remainder of URC
stories.summary: Eben Etzebeth's return to the Sharks has been cut short due to a hip injury, ruling him out for the remainder of the United Rugby Championship (URC) season. The injury occurred during his first game back, where he played under an hour. Etzebeth's focus will now shift to recovering in time for the Springbok season, which begins on July 4 against England in Johannesburg. The Sharks have also been dealt further blows with injuries to fellow Springboks Bongi Mbonambi, Grant Williams, and Jordan Hendrikse, who will also miss the rest of the URC season. Etzebeth will miss the Sharks' remaining four regular season matches and potential playoffs, ending his season with the team prematurely. The injury comes after Etzebeth recently completed a 12-week ban for gouging.

stories.headline: [The Times of India] 'We all love you', Trump tells PM Modi during 40-min call on Iran war, Hormuz
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump and Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi held a 40-minute phone call to discuss the situation in West Asia. Trump briefed Modi on the current state of the Middle East, focusing on the US blockade in the Strait of Hormuz. The two leaders stressed the importance of keeping the Strait of Hormuz open and secure. The call came after Trump ordered a blockade of ships entering or leaving Iranian ports and coastal areas in the Gulf. This was their third phone conversation this year, and Trump also expressed his affection for Modi, telling him "we all love you". The conversation reviewed bilateral relations and the evolving situation in the region, with the US Ambassador to India confirming the discussion on the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: [ThePrint] Modi, Trump discuss need to keep Strait of Hormuz open & secure after Iran hints at ‘new mechanism’
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump and Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi discussed the need to keep the Strait of Hormuz open and secure in a call. The conversation came 48 hours after US-Iran peace talks in Islamabad failed to yield a solution. The Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed due to a US blockade after Iran peace talks broke down. Both leaders emphasized the importance of a secure strait, addressing India's concerns about its ships. Modi affirmed India's support for a dialogue-based solution, prioritizing safe passage for Indian ships and the diaspora. The call follows a previous discussion between the two leaders after the Iran war began, with Trump suggesting joint US-Iran management and ongoing negotiations to resolve the conflict. India seeks a ceasefire and a secure Strait of Hormuz to ensure safe passage.

stories.headline: [The Hindu] Opposition support crucial to pass women’s reservation and delimitation Bills
stories.summary: The Indian government is seeking to pass the women's reservation and delimitation Bills, but requires opposition support to do so. The Bills aim to reserve 33% of seats for women in the Lok Sabha and state assemblies. A joint statement by activists, academics, and retired civil servants has called for the release of draft texts and public consultations before the special session of Parliament on April 16. The government has reached out to the opposition for input on amending the Women's Reservation Bill, 2023. The Congress party has expressed support for the immediate implementation of women's reservation. The passage of the Bills is crucial, but concerns over federal balance and clarity need to be addressed. The government and opposition are working together to evolve a strategy to ensure the Bills' passage during the upcoming Parliament session.

stories.headline: [The Times of India] What triggered Vedanta power plant explosion? Questions mount after 13 deaths in Chhattisgarh
stories.summary: An explosion at a Vedanta power plant in Chhattisgarh's Sakti district has resulted in 13 deaths and injured over 40 people. The incident occurred in the coal-fired boiler system, with police stating that a tube rupture led to a high-pressure steam leak, triggering the blast. Preliminary findings suggest that the bursting of a pipeline carrying hot water caused severe burn injuries among workers. The explosion is believed to have been caused by a combination of factors, including a possible gas leak. An investigation is underway to determine the exact cause of the incident. The power plant, located in the Sakti district, is a major industrial facility in the region. The incident has raised questions about the safety measures in place at the plant and the measures being taken to prevent such accidents in the future.

stories.headline: Xi meets UAE Crown Prince MBZ in Beijing, pitches 4-point proposal for stability in West Asia
stories.summary: President Xi Jinping met with Sheikh Khaled bin Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan, Crown Prince of Abu Dhabi, at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing. Xi emphasized China's commitment to promoting peace and stability in the Middle East, reaffirming China's constructive role in the region. He presented a four-point proposal to achieve this goal, stressing adherence to peaceful coexistence. The meeting highlights the deepening partnership between China and the UAE, which extends beyond trade to geopolitics and regional security. The UAE is a comprehensive strategic partner of China, and Xi noted that China highly values this bilateral relationship. China has become one of the UAE's largest economic partners, and the talks signal a growing collaboration on regional stability and security issues. Xi's proposal aims to promote peace and stability in West Asia.

stories.headline: The Philippines today finds itself caught in the crossfire of a global crisis not of its own making. War in the Middle East has disrupted oil supply routes,
stories.summary: The Philippines has declared a national energy emergency due to the war in the Middle East, which has disrupted oil supply routes and driven up crude prices. President Ferdinand Marcos stated that the situation poses an "imminent danger" to the country. The Philippines is heavily dependent on external energy sources, with the war exposing its vulnerability. The instability in the Strait of Hormuz, a key oil trade route, has led to a sharp rise in oil prices. The country is the first in Southeast Asia to declare a national energy emergency, highlighting its susceptibility to global energy market fluctuations. The move is seen as a response to the crisis rather than a demonstration of leadership, with analysts noting that the Philippines' energy dependence is a key driver of its strategic choices.

stories.headline: Oil seen vulnerable as IMF meetings may cement view Iran conflict is over, Brooks says
stories.summary: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) meetings next week are expected to focus on the Iran conflict's impact on energy flows, supply chains, food security, and inflation. Finance ministers, central bankers, and CEOs will gather in Washington to discuss the conflict's effects. The IMF views the conflict as a major supply shock that will test global resilience. Even if the conflict ends quickly, the IMF anticipates cutting growth forecasts and raising inflation projections. A short conflict could lead to a temporary surge in oil and gas prices, while a prolonged conflict could keep energy costs high and strain import-reliant countries. The meetings may cement the view that the conflict is ending, potentially stripping the geopolitics premium from crude and leading to lower oil prices. The IMF will offer multiple projections on the conflict's economic implications.

stories.headline: Geopolitics rattles Pakistan stocks: KSE-100 crashes 4,800 points
stories.summary: The Pakistan Stock Exchange (PSX) experienced a significant fluctuation, with the KSE-100 index crashing 4,800 points due to geopolitical tensions. However, the index later rebounded, gaining over 1,400 points amid hopes of easing tensions. In a dramatic turn, the KSE-100 index jumped over 12,000 points in intraday trade, marking its biggest single-day gain of 8.15 percent. The index rose to 164,035.83, driven primarily by banking stocks, including MCB, HBL, UBL, and Bank of Punjab. These heavyweights contributed 552 points to the index, supporting the upward trajectory. The volatility in the PSX is attributed to geopolitical developments, including the impact of the US presidential election, which previously caused the KSE-100 index to shed nearly 800 points after Donald Trump's victory. The recent gains indicate a positive response to easing geopolitical tensions.

stories.headline: Why is stock market rising today? Sensex soars 1,300 points, Nifty above 24,200. 5 key factors explained
stories.summary: Indian stock markets rallied on Wednesday, with the Sensex and Nifty rising over 1.5% each. The Sensex gained 1,217.88 points to reach 82,548.44, and the NSE Nifty50 climbed 410.57 points to 25,077.50. The gains were driven by easing oil prices, renewed hopes of US-Iran peace talks, and strong performances from shares of HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, and Reliance Industries. Buying was seen across segments, and risk appetite improved, with the India VIX falling 11. The market capitalisation increased by over Rs 7.5 lakh crore. HCL Tech also contributed to the rise, climbing 6.45% to Rs 1,575.60 after releasing in-line Q4 results. The overall market sentiment was positive, leading to significant gains in the stock market benchmarks.

stories.headline: Ahead of Market: 10 things that will decide stock market action on Wednesday
stories.summary: Indian markets closed higher on Tuesday, driven by gains in HDFC Bank and Reliance Industries, amid expectations of a US Federal Reserve rate cut. The Sensex rose 597.67 points and Nifty gained 181.10 points, led by banking and metal stocks. However, on Friday, Indian markets closed lower due to weak global cues, renewed US trade tariffs, and sustained FII outflows, with Sensex falling 586 points and Nifty 203. European stocks climbed on Friday as disappointing data on China's manufacturing sector spurred hopes of extra policy stimulus. Meanwhile, US markets saw a relief rally after President Donald Trump retreated on tariffs on European nations. The pan-Eu STOXX 600 index rose, and Wall Street stocks opened higher on Thursday, extending the relief rally. Global market trends and interest rate decisions will continue to influence stock market action.

stories.headline: Stock Market Outlook Tomorrow: After Market Crash Will Nifty, Sensex Rally Tomorrow?
stories.summary: The Indian stock market experienced a significant decline, with the Sensex dropping 931 points and the Nifty falling 222 points due to a surge in oil prices. This downturn has raised concerns about the market's outlook for the next day. However, global markets have shown signs of recovery after President Trump announced a US-Iran ceasefire, leading to a decrease in crude oil prices and an increase in equities. The Brent and WTI crude prices have fallen below $100, indicating a potential positive impact on the market. The stock market's performance is closely watched, with investors seeking updates on share prices, Sensex, and Nifty live indices. The latest trends and analysis are being monitored to predict the market's movement, including intraday and long-term strategies, and stock tips. The market's rally tomorrow will depend on various factors, including global market trends and economic indicators.

stories.headline: Sensex, Nifty rally tomorrow? Why Gift Nifty jumped 260 pts today Stock market outlook for Wednesday
stories.summary: The Indian stock market experienced a significant rally, with the Sensex jumping 2,600 points and the Nifty nearing 24,000. This surge was driven by a global equity rally, cooled oil prices, and a boost to the rupee following US-Iran ceasefire talks. However, heavyweight stocks in the IT and auto sectors declined by 1% and 1.41%, respectively. The Sensex opened lower than its previous close but dropped further to a day's low of 75,294.76, losing over 600 points, before rebounding. The RBI's decision to hold the repo rate at 5.25% also contributed to the market's positive performance. The Gift Nifty jumped 260 points, and analysts predict a potential rally tomorrow, citing reduced probability of immediate peak escalation. The market's outlook for Wednesday is positive, with expectations of continued gains.

stories.headline: Amazon to buy satellite firm Globalstar for $11.57bn in challenge to Musk’s Starlink
stories.summary: Amazon will acquire Globalstar, a satellite company, in an $11.57 billion deal. The acquisition aims to bolster Amazon's satellite business, enabling it to compete with Elon Musk's Starlink. Globalstar's satellites, radio frequency spectrum, and operational expertise will allow Amazon to add Direct-to-Device (D2D) services to its low Earth orbit satellite network, Amazon Leo. Globalstar is known for powering Apple's "Emergency SOS" feature. The deal is valued at $11.57 billion in cash, marking a significant investment in Amazon's fledgling space business. The acquisition is expected to enhance Amazon's capabilities in the satellite industry, posing a challenge to Starlink's dominance. The move demonstrates Amazon's commitment to expanding its presence in the global satellite market.

stories.headline: Guinness hits N15.7 billion Q1 profit, declares interim dividend
stories.summary: Guinness Nigeria Plc has reported a profit after tax of ₦10.39 billion for the quarter ended March 31, 2026. The company's profit before tax stood at ₦15.74 billion, representing a 53.19% year-over-year increase. Earnings per share rose to 474 kobo from 321 kobo in the corresponding period. Revenue growth was accompanied by a rise in the cost of sales to ₦79.2 billion, up from ₦73.8 billion. Following the strong financial performance, Guinness Nigeria has declared an interim dividend of ₦2.00 per ordinary share. The company's balance sheet has strengthened, underscoring a rebound in earnings and renewed focus on shareholder value. The interim dividend declaration reflects the company's commitment to delivering value to its shareholders.

stories.headline: Rio2 Limited (RIO:CA) Presents at Mining Forum Europe 2026 Transcript
stories.summary: Rio2 Limited, a Canadian junior mining company, presented at the 35th BMO Global Metals, Mining & Critical Minerals Conference on February 25, 2026. The company is transitioning from a developer to a producer, driven by the conversion of its Fenix Gold project from construction to operation, which occurred in January 2026. Rio2 Limited also presented at the Mining Forum Europe 2026 conference, although it was Royal Gold, Inc.'s management that announced their presentation, scheduled for April 14, 2026, in Zurich, Switzerland. Rio2's presentation at the BMO conference was made available as a prepared remarks transcript. The company's stock price and financial information are available, along with the latest news on Rio2 Limited, listed on the TSX under the symbol RIO.

stories.headline: Ecora Royalties PLC (ECOR:CA) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
stories.summary: Ecora Royalties PLC (ECOR:CA) held its Q4 2025 earnings call on April 14, 2026, at 10:00 AM EDT. Company participants included Marc Lafleche, CEO and Executive Director, and Kevin Flynn. The call discussed the company's financial performance for the fourth quarter of 2025. A transcript of the call is available, along with an investor presentation and earnings call slides. The presentation and slides provide additional information on the company's results and outlook. The earnings call was also held previously on March 26, 2026, with participants including Geoff Callow, Head of Investor Relations, and Marc Lafleche. The call transcripts, slides, and related documents are available for download, providing a comprehensive summary of Ecora Royalties' Q4 2025 performance.
